childNodes.textContent:
Kitsoki mesa-sun favicon, inlined as a data URI: the SPA is built as a
         single go:embed'd file (vite-plugin-singlefile), so an external
         /favicon.svg reference would not be served. Source of truth:
         docs/branding/assets/mesa-sun-simple.svg (the small-size glyph).

Kitsoki "Mesa Sun" mark (docs/branding/logo.md, mesa-sun-simple.svg).
         Inlined as an SVG data URI so it survives vite-plugin-singlefile's
         single-file bundle and ships inside index.html — the Go web server
         (internal/runstatus/server) only routes "/" and "/rpc/*", so an
         external /favicon.svg would 404.

node.textContent: An architect picks up a PRD

Phase 1 published the speaker-notes-export PRD on the slidey repo. Now an architect carries it into design — talking it into an epic, slices, ADRs, and a mermaid data-flow, no LLM in the loop. This session opens already at the published PRD, ready for the design handoff.

This is slidey-dev: kitsoki's dev-story hub pointed at the real slidey checkout. The published PRD sits at docs/prd/speaker-notes-export.md; the design will land at docs/proposals/speaker-notes-export.md.
Click New session. The run opens already at the published-PRD handoff — the design phase's starting line — so the architect picks up exactly where phase 1 left off.
↳ Click the highlighted control to continue.
A PRD is ready for design

The architect's session opens on the published speaker-notes-export PRD — phase 1's validated output, sitting at docs/prd/speaker-notes-export.md in the slidey tree. `continue` carries it into the design phase, seeded with a pointer to the PRD so the design author reads it as prior art, not a blank slate.

Author the design — epic, slices, ADRs

The brief passes the quality gate and the design author writes the full slidey runtime design: an epic (a spec-derived per-scene handout), three slices (a pure collectNotes(spec), the --notes CLI seam, a Markdown emitter), a mermaid spec→collectNotes→renderNotesMarkdown→out.md data-flow, and two ADRs (reuse the scene model so notes never drift; keep the transform pure and LLM-free).

node.textContent:
## Runtime: slidey `--notes` speaker-notes export

Realizes the speaker-notes-export PRD as a deterministic,
spec-derived output alongside video/PDF/web.

### Epic
Emit a per-scene Markdown handout from a deck spec, reusing the
same scene model the renderers share so it can never drift.

### Slices
1. `collectNotes(spec)` — a pure spec→notes collector in
   `src/notes.js` that walks `spec.scenes` and pulls eyebrow/title,
   body, and narration per scene.
2. CLI seam — `slidey deck.json --notes out.md` in `src/index.js`,
   routed like the existing `--pdf` / video output branches.
3. Markdown emitter — `renderNotesMarkdown(notes)` writing one
   `## NN — <title>` section per scene, narration as a blockquote.

### Data flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  spec[deck.json spec] --> collect[collectNotes]
  collect --> notes[scene notes]
  notes --> md[renderNotesMarkdown]
  md --> out[out.md handout]
```

### ADRs
- ADR-1: Derive notes from the SAME `spec.scenes` model the Vue
  scene components consume — no parallel notes schema.
- ADR-2: Notes export is a pure, LLM-free transform, matching
  slidey's deterministic same-input-same-output contract.

Accept publishes the design to the slidey tree at docs/proposals/speaker-notes-export.md and auto-mints a feature ticket at issues/features/F-<timestamp>-speaker-notes-export.md, linking back to the design — the implementation pipeline can pick it up immediately. That's phase 2: a published PRD, talked into a slidey design with an epic, slices, ADRs, and a mermaid diagram, all conversation-driven, no LLM in the loop.

ADR-1: Project over the parsed spec, do not re-render
, so coupling to the renderer (Puppeteer, timing) would add cost and nondeterminism for no benefit.

ADR-2: Emit every scene, mark empties
A reviewer must see one section per scene. Silently dropping narration-less scenes hides structure. *

Output must diff cleanly in review and be identical across machines. No timestamps, no map iteration order, no locale formatting.
Decision: deterministic string assembly in spec order.
